flags: 2006-07: Magic went 40-42 (losing record) but made the playoffs as 8th seed in the East; confirmed lost to Detroit Pistons 0-4 in First Round -- counts as firstRoundLoss not winningRecordNoPlayoff
2019-20: Bubble season; Magic finished 33-40 (losing record) but participated in playoffs as existing playoff seed; lost to Milwaukee Bucks 4-1 in First Round
2024-25 and 2025-26: Magic entered playoffs via play-in tournament; per rules only the 16-team bracket result is counted (both were First Round eliminations)
Three seasons (1992-93, 1997-98, 1999-00) finished exactly 41-41 (.500) and missed playoffs -- not counted as winningRecordNoPlayoff since rule requires strictly greater than .500
teams.notes: Franchise founded 1989, has never won an NBA title. Two Finals appearances (1995 swept by Rockets, 2009 lost to Lakers in 5). Two Conference Finals exits (1996 vs Bulls, 2010 vs Celtics). One Conference Semis exit (2008 vs Pistons). The remaining 14 playoff appearances all ended in the First Round. No seasons with a winning record and no playoff berth -- every missed-playoff year had a losing or exactly .500 record.

flags: 1997-98 and 2015-16 records were exactly 41-41 (.500), not counted as winning-record-no-playoff since rule requires strictly >.500
2023-24 was also exactly 41-41 and team missed both play-in and playoffs
2011-12 was the lockout-shortened 66-game season; 34-32 (.515) counts as winning record
Play-in tournament appearances (if any post-2021) not counted as playoff rounds per PPI rules
2025-26 season data sourced from Wikipedia; current date is June 2026 post-playoffs
2016-17 season was exactly 41-41 (.500), not counted as winning record (rule requires >.500)
2022-23 and 2024-25 Finals/first-round appearances were reached via play-in tournament; play-in losses are excluded per rules, only bracket-proper eliminations counted
2025-26: Heat lost in play-in to Charlotte Hornets (OT), so missed playoffs; 43-39 winning record counts as winningRecordNoPlayoff
teams.notes: Heat last won in 2013 (LeBron era). Since then: 3 Finals losses (2014 to Spurs, 2020 to Lakers, 2023 to Nuggets), 1 Conference Finals loss (2022 to Celtics), 1 Conference Semifinals loss (2016 to Raptors), 4 first-round losses (2018 vs 76ers, 2021 vs Bucks, 2024 vs Celtics, 2025 vs Cavaliers sweep), and 1 winning-record playoff miss (2025-26 via play-in loss).

flags: 1983-84 team made playoffs with a 35-47 losing record (8th seed in expanded East bracket)
2020-21 first round loss was via play-in qualification; loss in 16-team bracket counted per rules
1982-83 (42-40) and 1997-98 (42-40) both had winning records and missed playoffs
teams.notes: Drought runs from 1978-79 through 2025-26 (47 seasons). The Wizards never reached the Conference Finals during the drought; their deepest runs were 5 Conference Semifinals losses. They made the playoffs with losing records in multiple seasons in the 1980s (1983-84 at 35-47, 1984-85 at 40-42, 1985-86 at 39-43, 1987-88 at 38-44). The 2015-16 season at exactly 41-41 (.500) does not qualify as a winning record under the greater-than-.500 rule.

flags: 2018-19 season: Pistons had exactly .500 record (41-41); since they made playoffs it counts as a first-round loss, not winningRecordNoPlayoff
2025-26 season included: Pistons eliminated in Conference Semifinals by Cavaliers on May 19 2026 (current date June 16 2026, season complete for Detroit)
teams.notes: finalsLoss: 2004-05 (vs Spurs). semisLosses: 2005-06 (vs Heat), 2006-07 (vs Cavaliers), 2007-08 (vs Celtics). quartersLoss: 2025-26 (vs Cavaliers, Game 7 May 19 2026). firstRoundLosses: 2008-09 (vs Cavaliers), 2015-16 (vs Cavaliers), 2018-19 (vs Bucks; 41-41 record, exactly .500 not >.500), 2024-25 (vs Knicks). Seasons 2009-10 through 2014-15 and 2016-17 through 2023-24 were all losing-record missed-playoff seasons. Play-in tournament (2021+) not counted; Pistons never reached the 16-team bracket in those years.

teams.since: Since team relocated to Los Angeles in 1984
flags: 2025-26 season: Clippers finished 9th in West with 42-40 record, lost in NBA play-in tournament -- counted as winningRecordNoPlayoff
1996-97: Clippers made playoffs with a 36-46 losing record (8th seed) -- still a first-round loss, not winningRecordNoPlayoff
San Diego Clippers playoff history (1978-84) excluded because relocation to LA is more recent than any title
2021-22 play-in loss confirmed via Wikipedia
teams.notes: Drought starts 1984-85, the Clippers' first season in LA (relocated from San Diego). Team has never won an NBA championship. Semis: 2020-21 Conference Finals loss. Quarters (Conference Semifinals losses): 2005-06, 2011-12, 2013-14, 2014-15, 2019-20. First Round losses: 1991-92, 1992-93, 1996-97, 2012-13, 2015-16, 2016-17, 2018-19, 2022-23, 2023-24, 2024-25. Winning record no playoff: 2017-18 (42-40, missed playoffs outright), 2021-22 (42-40, lost play-in), 2025-26 (42-40, finished 9th West, lost play-in). Play-in losses (2021-22, 2025-26) classified as missed playoffs per PPI rules. The 1996-97 team had a losing 36-46 record but qualified as 8th seed and is counted as a first-round loss. Pre-LA seasons (Buffalo Braves 1970-78, San Diego Clippers 1978-84) excluded per relocation rule.

flags: 2021-22 and 2022-23: Timberwolves entered playoffs via play-in tournament; play-in games not counted per rules, only the first-round bracket loss counted
1998-99 was a lockout-shortened 50-game season; 25-25 record is exactly .500 (not a winning record), but they made the playoffs and lost in the first round
teams.notes: Timberwolves have never won an NBA title. Drought covers all 36 seasons from 1989-90 through 2025-26. Three Conference Finals appearances (2003-04 lost to Lakers, 2023-24 lost to Mavericks, 2024-25 lost to Thunder). 2025-26 ended with a Conference Semifinals loss to the Spurs. 2021-22 and 2022-23 playoff entries came via play-in tournament but losses occurred in the 16-team bracket first round so they count as first round losses. The only winning-record-no-playoff season was 2004-05 (44-38).

10-season drought (2016-17 through 2025-26). 2021-22: Cavaliers went 44-38 but lost in the NBA play-in tournament to both the Nets and Hawks; since the play-in is not counted as a playoff round, this registers as winning-record-no-playoff. 2016-17 Conference Finals loss was to the Celtics. 2017-18 Finals loss was to the Warriors. 2022-23 first-round loss was to the Knicks. 2023-24 Conference Semifinals loss was to the Celtics. 2024-25 Conference Semifinals loss (4-1) was to the Pacers. 2025-26 Conference Finals loss was a sweep (0-4) to the Knicks.

teams.since: Since team relocated to Memphis in 2001
flags: Play-in 2021: Grizzlies beat Warriors in play-in to become 8-seed, then lost to Jazz 4-1 in proper first round -- counted as firstRoundLoss per rules
2019-20 bubble season: 34-39 record, below .500, no playoffs -- nothing
Vancouver Grizzlies history (1995-2001) excluded per relocation rule
teams.notes: Semis loss: 2012-13 Conference Finals (Spurs). Quarters losses: 2010-11 (Thunder), 2014-15 (Warriors), 2021-22 (Warriors). First round losses: 2003-04 (Spurs), 2004-05 (Suns), 2005-06 (Mavericks), 2011-12 (Clippers), 2013-14 (Thunder), 2015-16 (Spurs), 2016-17 (Spurs), 2020-21 (Jazz), 2022-23 (Lakers), 2024-25 (Thunder). No winning-record-no-playoff seasons in Memphis era.

flags: 2022-23 and 2024-25: Bulls entered play-in tournament but were eliminated before the 16-team bracket; per rules these seasons are NOT counted as playoff appearances
2015-16: 42-40 winning record but missed playoffs (8th seed cutoff was 45 wins); counted as winningRecordNoPlayoff
2019-20 was a COVID-shortened season (22-43 in 65 games played); losing record, no playoff bubble appearance
teams.notes: Drought covers 1998-99 through 2025-26 (27 seasons). The one Conference Finals appearance (2010-11, 62-win team) and eight first-round exits define the drought. No Finals appearances since the dynasty ended.

flags: 2024-25: Dallas (39-43) lost in the NBA Play-In Tournament and did NOT enter the 16-team bracket proper -- counted as missed playoffs per PPI rules
2012-13: 41-41 record is exactly .500, not >.500, so not counted as winning-record-missed-playoffs
teams.notes: 6 first-round exits dominated the drought until 2021-22 (Conference Finals loss to Warriors) and 2023-24 (Finals loss to Celtics). 2024-25 Mavericks (post-Luka trade) went 39-43 and lost in the play-in to Memphis, never reaching the bracket. 2025-26 was a 26-56 rebuild season.

teams.since: Since franchise relocated to Sacramento in 1985
flags: Play-in losses (2023-24 vs. New Orleans Pelicans; 2024-25 vs. Dallas Mavericks) excluded per rules -- Kings never entered 16-team bracket those years
2023-24: 46-36 winning record counted as winningRecordNoPlayoff since loss occurred in play-in only
Drought start anchored to 1985 relocation to Sacramento (more recent than 1951 title)
teams.notes: First-round losses: 1985-86 (HOU), 1995-96 (SEA), 1998-99 (UTA), 1999-00 (LAL), 2004-05 (SEA), 2005-06 (SAS), 2022-23 (GSW). Conference Semis losses: 2000-01 (LAL), 2002-03 (DAL), 2003-04 (MIN). Conference Finals loss: 2001-02 (LAL, famous Game 7). 2025-26 season confirmed 22-60, missed playoffs entirely.

12-season drought (2014-15 through 2025-26). The six missed-playoff seasons (2019-20 through 2024-25) all had losing records so none count toward winningRecordNoPlayoff. The 2025-26 Finals loss to the Knicks (1-4) is the most painful result of the drought. The 2014-15 first-round exit to the Clippers (Game 7) is confirmed by Basketball-Reference. The 2015-16 Conference Semifinals loss to OKC (67-win Spurs knocked out in Round 2) is verified by Wikipedia.

flags: Franchise identity is complex: the original Charlotte Hornets (1988-2002) relocated to New Orleans (now the Pelicans); the current franchise began as the Charlotte Bobcats in 2004-05 and was renamed Hornets in 2014, officially adopting the 1988 history. PPI is computed from 1988 per official NBA franchise history. An alternative start of 2004-05 (Bobcats founding) would yield quartersLosses=0, firstRoundLosses=3, winningRecordNoPlayoff=2.
Charlotte had no NBA team for two seasons (2002-03 and 2003-04) after the original Hornets relocated to New Orleans; those seasons are excluded from PPI counting.
1998-99 season shortened to 50 games (NBA lockout); Hornets went 26-24 (.520) and finished 9th in the East, missing playoffs — counted as winningRecordNoPlayoff.
2021-22 (43-39) and 2025-26 (44-38) both had winning records and play-in appearances but did not reach the 16-team bracket proper; counted as winningRecordNoPlayoff per rules.
quartersLosses are all Conference Semifinal exits (1993, 1998, 2001, 2002); the team never advanced past the second round in any season.
teams.notes: The four Conference Semifinal losses (1993, 1998, 2001, 2002) all came from the original Charlotte Hornets era. The franchise has never advanced past the second round. Six first-round exits span three eras: original Hornets (1995, 1997, 2000), Bobcats (2010, 2014), and modern Hornets (2016). The three winning-record no-playoff seasons are 1998-99 (lockout year), 2021-22, and 2025-26 (both play-in exits).

teams.since: Since team relocated to New Orleans in 2002
teams.lastChampionship: Never won (relocated to New Orleans in 2002)
flags: 2003-04 (41-41 exactly .500) made the playoffs as Eastern Conference 5th seed; treated as firstRoundLoss not winningRecordNoPlayoff since they made the bracket
2021-22 team had losing record (36-46) but made the 16-team bracket via play-in; counts as firstRoundLoss per rules
2019-20 shortened COVID season; Pelicans finished 30-42 and missed the bubble/playoffs
teams.notes: Franchise relocated from Charlotte to New Orleans in 2002-03; drought starts from first New Orleans season. The team was in the Eastern Conference for 2002-03 and 2003-04 before moving to the Western Conference. The two quarter-final losses are both Conference Semifinals exits (2007-08 vs Spurs, 2017-18 vs Warriors). The 2022-23 season (42-40 winning record) counts as winningRecordNoPlayoff because the team was eliminated in the Play-In tournament and never entered the 16-team bracket. The 2021-22 season (36-46 losing record) entered via play-in and lost in the First Round proper, so it counts as a firstRoundLoss. The team has never reached the Conference Finals.

teams.lastChampionship: 1976 ABA Championship (as New York Nets; predates Brooklyn relocation)
flags: 2019-20 season shortened by COVID; Nets finished 35-37 (losing record) but qualified for bubble playoffs as 7th seed and were swept by Raptors in first round
2021-22: Nets entered playoffs via play-in win over Cavaliers; play-in itself not counted per rules, only the first-round loss to Celtics (swept 0-4)
teams.notes: Drought window begins 2012-13 (first Brooklyn season). The 1976 ABA title was won as the New York Nets, which predates the 2012 relocation; per rules the drought start is the relocation year. The franchise has never won an NBA championship. All 6 playoff appearances in the window ended in first-round (4) or second-round/Conference Semifinals (2) exits. No seasons with a winning record that missed the playoffs.

2021-22 was the only non-playoff season (33-49, losing record). 2022-23 Conference Finals loss to Denver (swept 0-4). 2023-24 and 2024-25 were first-round exits. 2025-26 lost to OKC Thunder in Conference Semifinals (swept 0-4). Play-in appearances in 2023-24 and 2025-26 were not counted as playoff rounds per rules.

Nuggets won their first-ever NBA championship in 2022-23 (defeated Miami Heat 4-1). Drought covers 3 seasons: 2023-24 (lost Conference Semifinals to Timberwolves 3-4), 2024-25 (lost Conference Semifinals to Thunder 3-4), 2025-26 (lost First Round to Timberwolves 2-4). All three seasons were playoff appearances with winning records, so winningRecordNoPlayoff = 0.

2021-22: Lost Conference Semifinals to Celtics (3-4). 2022-23: Lost First Round to Miami Heat (1-4) as the #1 seed. 2023-24: Lost First Round to Indiana Pacers (2-4). 2024-25: Lost First Round to Indiana Pacers (1-4) as the #5 seed, direct playoff entry (no play-in). 2025-26: 32-50 losing record, missed playoffs entirely -- first losing season since 2015-16.

Thunder won their first OKC-era championship in 2024-25 (defeated Pacers 4-3). The active drought covers only 2025-26, in which they went 64-18 and lost the Western Conference Finals to the Spurs 4-3.

2022-23 record was exactly 41-41 (.500), which does not qualify as a winning record (rule requires >.500). The 2020-21 season (27-45) and 2023-24 (25-57) and 2024-25 (30-52) were all losing-record missed-playoff seasons that contribute nothing to PPI.

flags: 2023-24 play-in loss confirmed (10th seed West, lost to Sacramento Kings or similar); play-in correctly excluded from playoff round counting per rules
2025-26 play-in loss to Phoenix Suns confirmed via Yahoo Sports; 37-45 is a losing record so no winningRecordNoPlayoff credit
teams.notes: 4 drought seasons (2022-23 through 2025-26). 2022-23: lost Conference Semifinals to LA Lakers (beat Kings 4-3 in first round). 2023-24: 46-36 winning record but lost in play-in tournament, missed 16-team bracket. 2024-25: lost First Round to Houston Rockets (blew 3-1 lead, series lost 3-4). 2025-26: 37-45 losing record, lost in play-in to Phoenix Suns, no points counted.

flags: 2025-26 First Round exit is notable: Celtics blew a 3-1 series lead to the 76ers, first time Philly overcame that deficit in franchise history
teams.notes: Celtics won the 2024 NBA Finals (beat Dallas Mavericks). In 2024-25, they finished 61-21 and lost to the New York Knicks 2-4 in the Conference Semifinals (maps to Quarter-finals). In 2025-26, they finished 56-26 but lost to the Philadelphia 76ers 3-4 in the First Round after blowing a 3-1 series lead; Jayson Tatum missed the first 62 games with a torn Achilles.

The Knicks won the 2026 NBA Finals, defeating the San Antonio Spurs 4-1 and ending a 53-year championship drought (their previous title was 1973). Jalen Brunson was named Finals MVP. The active drought is 0 — no seasons have elapsed since the title. The drought window starts with the 2026-27 season.
